title: research paper / monetary policy transmission / credit card spending | Research Paper | How Interest Rate Changes Affect Credit Card Spending
summary: Using supervisory data covering nearly 80 percent of US credit card accounts active during 2016-2025, Federal Reserve Bank of Boston researchers find that a 1 percentage point increase in credit card APR reduces credit card spending by approximately 8.7 percent in the following month. The effect is concentrated among revolvers (borrowers carrying month-to-month balances), who reduce spending by about 15 percent per 1pp APR increase, while transactors who pay balances in full show no significant response. Lower-credit-score borrowers also exhibit roughly double the sensitivity of higher-credit-score borrowers to interest rate changes.
